Title: CcPmk1 is a regulator of pathogenicity in Cytospora chrysosperma and can be used as a potential target for disease control

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Fus3/Kss1/Pmk1 MAPK; protein kinase domain PF00069; conserved TEY phosphorylation motif
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: MAPK signalling pathway; simulated infection conditions with poplar twigs
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive regulation of downstream transcription factors, putative effector genes, and secondary metabolism gene clusters; controls pathogenicity-associated transcriptional programs
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene deletion mutant transcriptome comparison (RNA-seq); RT-qPCR after asiRNA silencing; in vitro asiRNA feeding; phenotype assay for fungal growth; cross-species TRV-VIGS assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion or silencing reduces fungal growth and pathogenicity-associated gene expression; CcPmk1 acts as a positive regulator of secondary metabolism clusters, transcription factors, and effector genes
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fungal secondary metabolism gene clusters in mycelia during simulated infection
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Secondary metabolism biosynthesis and broader pathogenicity-associated gene regulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 11 secondary metabolite core biosynthesis genes; terpene cluster GME3317_g-GME3324_g; hybrid-NRPS/T1PKS cluster GME3437_g-GME3444_g; multiple transcription factor genes; putative effector genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates/maintains expression of many secondary metabolism core genes and clusters; 11 of 68 core secondary metabolism biosynthesis genes were significantly downregulated in the deletion mutant
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Global regulator linking MAPK signalling to pathogenicity-associated secondary metabolism and virulence outputs
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Function assessed during mimetic infection process in PDB supplemented with poplar twigs
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Compared between wild type and ΔCcPmk1 during simulated infection
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions within the MAPK signalling cascade; specific complex members not experimentally defined here
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Simulated infection with sterilized poplar twigs; in vitro asiRNA treatment; TRV-based VIGS in Nicotiana benthamiana for cross-species silencing context
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; NCBI SRA: SRR12262932, SRR12262933, SRR12262934, SRR12262935, SRR12262936, SRR12262937; reference genome accession JAEQMF000000000
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential broad-spectrum disease-control target via RNAi/HIGS/VIGS-based silencing, but off-target effects on nonpathogenic or beneficial fungi should be minimized
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Off-target silencing risks to host plants and beneficial/nonpathogenic fungi due to high sequence conservation among Pmk1 homologs
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Study is mainly about pathogenicity, not a nutrient-quality trait. Secondary metabolism effects are broad and cluster-level; specific metabolite identities were mostly not resolved. Ribosome biogenesis and rRNA processing were also strongly affected downstream of CcPmk1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CcPmk1 is a conserved Fus3/Kss1-type MAPK required for fungal growth and pathogenicity in Cytospora chrysosperma. 2. RNA-seq showed 11 of 68 core secondary metabolism biosynthesis genes and several associated clusters were significantly downregulated in the ΔCcPmk1 mutant. 3. CcPmk1 also positively regulates many transcription factor and putative effector genes during infection-mimicking conditions. 4. asiRNA silencing of CcPmk1 inhibited fungal growth, supporting its utility as a disease-control target.

Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: TRV-based VIGS using conserved CcPmk1 or BcPmk1 fragments in Nicotiana benthamiana followed by Botrytis cinerea infection
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TRV-based virus-induced gene silencing in Nicotiana benthamiana; lesion area measurement after Botrytis cinerea inoculation; RT-qPCR of BcPmk1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing BcPmk1 reduces B. cinerea virulence on N. benthamiana and lowers BcPmk1 transcript levels
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Decisive for virulence, but no nutrient-metabolite endpoint was directly tested
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Measured in B. cinerea recovered from infected N. benthamiana leaves
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TRV-based VIGS in N. benthamiana followed by B. cinerea infection
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports cross-species RNAi-based targeting of conserved fungal Pmk1 homologs for broad-spectrum disease control
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Conservation raises off-target risks in non-target fungi
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is directly experimentally tested in Results, but it is not linked to nutrient metabolism in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Conserved CcPmk1/BcPmk1 fragments delivered through TRV-VIGS reduced BcPmk1 expression in Botrytis cinerea infecting Nicotiana benthamiana. 2. Reduced BcPmk1 expression significantly compromised fungal virulence. 3. This supports conserved Pmk1 homologs as disease-control targets.
